name: airport_facial_mismatch_citizen_detained_connection
premise: TSA federal security director Avery Cole must decide whether to keep biometric one-to-many matching active after three false positives detained citizens who missed international connections last week. Civil rights attorney Priya Desai demands the feature paused until NIST-style demographic parity testing completes. Intelligence liaison warns of an imminent coordinated threat using stolen passports at this hub. Pausing matching may speed lines but removes a layer catching lookalike fraud rings.

agents.name: Avery Cole
agents.identity: I am the federal security director accountable for threat detection, lawful screening, and minimizing discriminatory disparate impact at checkpoints.
agents.goal: I want effective interdiction without normalizing wrongful detention patterns that destroy trust in federal screening.

agents.name: Priya Desai
agents.identity: I am a civil rights attorney representing wrongfully detained travelers and negotiating emergency policy changes with airport legal counsel.
agents.goal: I want biometric one-to-many matching paused until independent parity evidence exists, even if that complicates threat interdiction temporarily.

choices: I will pause one-to-many matching for forty-eight hours and surge human document experts, accepting longer queues and intelligence friction during the window.
I will keep matching active but add immediate secondary supervisor review for any match below a raised confidence threshold, accepting that some threats may slip if thresholds are too high.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Homeland Security is requesting a written decision before the morning bank of European departures. Avery Cole must choose whether to pause biometric matching or keep it with elevated review. What does Avery Cole do?
